result.markdown:
--- title: Geocoding --- ::: {.cell} ::: ### {tidygeocoder} ![](assets/tidygeocoder.png) ### {tidygeocoder} {.inverse} ### Geocoding a Full Address ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) famous_places <- tribble( ~building , ~address , "White House" , "1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20500 United States" , "Empire State Building" , "350 5th Ave, New York, NY 10118, USA" ) ``` ::: ### Geocoding a Full Address ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidygeocoder) famous_places |> geocode(address) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 4 building address lat long 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20… 38.9 -77.0 2 Empire State Building 350 5th Ave, New York, NY 10118, USA 40.7 -74.0 ``` ::: ::: ### Geocoding a Full Address ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} famous_places_sf <- famous_places |> geocode(address) |> st_as_sf( coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326 ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} famous_places_sf ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Simple feature collection with 2 features and 2 fields Geometry type: POINT Dimension: XY Bounding box: xmin: -77.03655 ymin: 38.89764 xmax: -73.98566 ymax: 40.74844 Geodetic CRS: WGS 84 # A tibble: 2 × 3 building address geometry * 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Was… (-77.03655 38.89764) 2 Empire State Building 350 5th Ave, New York, NY 101… (-73.98566 40.74844) ``` ::: ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} famous_places_sf |> mapview::mapview() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Geocoding Services {.inverse} ::: {.notes} Show getting API key from LocationIQ ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} us_and_uk_head_residences <- tribble( ~building , ~address , "White House" , "1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20500 United States" , "Number 10" , "10 Downing St, London SW1A 2AA, United Kingdom" ) us_and_uk_head_residences ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 2 building address 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20500 United States 2 Number 10 10 Downing St, London SW1A 2AA, United Kingdom ``` ::: ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} us_and_uk_head_residences |> geocode(address) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 4 building address lat long 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20500 Unit… 38.9 -77.0 2 Number 10 10 Downing St, London SW1A 2AA, United Kingdom 51.5 -0.128 ``` ::: ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="4"} us_and_uk_head_residences |> geocode( address, method = "iq" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 4 building address lat long 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington, DC 20500 Unit… 38.9 -77.0 2 Number 10 10 Downing St, London SW1A 2AA, United Kingdom 51.5 -0.128 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.notes} Both ArcGIS (https://location.arcgis.com/pricing/#geocoding) and Mapbox (https://www.mapbox.com/pricing) are paid for storing addresses ::: --- ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} us_and_uk_head_residences_v2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 6 building address city state postal_code country 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington DC 20500 United Stat… 2 Number 10 10 Downing St London SW1A 2AA United King… ``` ::: ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} us_and_uk_head_residences_v2 |> geocode( street = address, city = city, state = state, postalcode = postal_code, country = country ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 8 building address city state postal_code country lat long 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave… Wash… DC 20500 United… 38.9 -77.0 2 Number 10 10 Downing St Lond… SW1A 2AA United… NA NA ``` ::: ::: ::: {.notes} Doesn't work for Number 10, which is transition to talking about `method` argument ::: --- ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="8"} us_and_uk_head_residences_v2 |> geocode( street = address, city = city, state = state, postalcode = postal_code, country = country, method = "iq" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 2 × 8 building address city state postal_code country lat long 1 White House 1600 Pennsylvania A… Wash… DC 20500 United… 38.9 -77.0 2 Number 10 10 Downing St Lond… SW1A 2AA United… 51.5 -0.128 ``` ::: ::: ### Your Turn {.your-turn} - Create a tibble with the following starter code for your home address: . . . ::: {.cell output-location='default'} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) my_address <- tribble( ~address , "Your address goes here" ) ``` ::: ### Your Turn {.your-turn} - Use the {tidygeocoder} package to geocode your address - Turn the result into an `sf` object with `st_as_sf()` and plot it on a map with `mapview::mapview()` to make sure it worked - Remember that if you want to use `method = "iq"` you'll need to sign up for a free LocationIQ account and create an API key

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
